_description: This database contains confirmed wasei-eigo terms that should be flagged for learners. Wasei-eigo are words that appear to be English but are actually Japanese coinages that don't exist in standard English, or have significantly different meanings.
_usage: Used by detect_wasei_eigo.py to automatically flag wasei-eigo in the curated dataset

アメリカンドッグ.notes: In English, this food item is called 'corn dog', not 'American dog'

バージョンアップ.notes: English speakers say 'update' or 'upgrade', not 'version up'

アウトコース.notes: In baseball English: 'outside pitch' or 'away'. In racing: 'outer lane'

サラリーマン.notes: Classic wasei-eigo. English speakers would say 'office worker' or 'white-collar worker'

ノートパソコン.notes: While 'notebook' exists in English, 'note pasokon' is a Japanese construction

バイキング.notes: In Japan, 'Viking' refers to buffet-style dining, not the Norse people

モーニングコール.notes: English speakers say 'wake-up call', not 'morning call'

コンセント.notes: Derived from 'concentric plug' but means 'outlet' in Japanese. English: 'outlet' or 'socket'

サインペン.notes: While understandable, English speakers say 'felt-tip pen' or 'marker'

ガソリンスタンド.notes: English: 'gas station' or 'petrol station' (UK), not 'gasoline stand'

パソコン.notes: Abbreviated form unique to Japanese. English speakers use 'PC' or spell out 'computer'

マイカー.notes: While grammatically correct, used as a noun in Japanese unlike English

ナイター.notes: Sports term. English speakers say 'night game', not 'nighter'

アフターサービス.notes: English would say 'after-sales service' or 'customer support'

クレーム.notes: From 'claim' but used to mean 'complaint' in Japanese. Semantic shift from English

patterns._comment: Regex patterns that suggest potential wasei-eigo

verb_up_down.note: Many 'X-up' or 'X-down' constructions are wasei-eigo. English uses different phrasing.

american_prefix.pattern: ^アメリカン(?!フットボール|リーグ)
american_prefix.note: 'American X' constructions often don't exist in English with that exact naming

my_prefix.note: 'My X' used as compound nouns is a Japanese pattern

english_validation._comment: Common English words/phrases to validate sourceWord against
english_validation._description: If sourceWord exists but is not in standard English dictionaries, it may be wasei-eigo
